assertion_results.text: The verification report marks all 5 acceptance criteria as PASS — the diff implements single license filter, comma-separated filter, 400 validation, pagination integration, and unchanged response shape
assertion_results.evidence: report.md shows 'Acceptance Criteria | PASS | 5 of 5 criteria met' in the summary table. The Acceptance Criteria Details table lists all 5 criteria with PASS verdicts and detailed reasoning. criterion-1.md through criterion-5.md each show 'Verdict: PASS' with code-level analysis covering single license filter, comma-separated filter, 400 validation, pagination integration, and unchanged response shape respectively.

assertion_results.text: Scope containment is PASS — all files in the diff (list.rs, service/mod.rs, tests/api/package.rs) are listed in the task's Files to Modify or Files to Create
assertion_results.evidence: report.md shows 'Scope Containment | PASS | PR modifies exactly the files specified in the task: `modules/fundamental/src/package/endpoints/list.rs`, `modules/fundamental/src/package/service/mod.rs` (modified), and `tests/api/package.rs` (created). No out-of-scope files, no unimplemented files.'

assertion_results.text: No sub-tasks are created because there are no review comments (Review Feedback is N/A)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md shows 'Review Feedback | N/A | No reviews or comments exist on the PR'. No sub-task creation is mentioned in any output file. Root-Cause Investigation row confirms 'No sub-tasks created; nothing to investigate'.

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT modify any code files — it only reads, verifies, and reports (constraint 1.11)
assertion_results.evidence: All output files are analysis/report documents: report.md and criterion-1.md through criterion-5.md. The criterion files describe code that was read and analyzed (e.g., 'The `validate_license_param()` function splits the license string by commas') but contain no indication of code modifications. No code files appear in the outputs directory.

assertion_results.text: The report does NOT auto-merge the PR — it presents findings for human review (constraint 1.13)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md concludes with 'Overall: PASS' and a summary of findings but takes no merge action. The report is signed 'This comment was AI-generated by sdlc-workflow/verify-pr v0.9.1', indicating it is a comment/report for human review, not an automated merge action.

assertion_results.text: Sensitive pattern scan is PASS — the diff contains no passwords, API keys, or private keys
assertion_results.evidence: report.md shows 'Sensitive Patterns | PASS | No secrets, credentials, API keys, or other sensitive patterns detected in added lines across all 3 files'.

assertion_results.text: The report contains a Test Change Classification row with ADDITIVE — only new test files were added (tests/api/package.rs is a new file)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md shows 'Test Change Classification | ADDITIVE | All test files are newly created; no existing tests were modified or removed'.

assertion_results.text: The verification report assembles verdicts from all four domain sub-agents: Scope Containment, Diff Size, and Commit Traceability from Intent Alignment; Sensitive Patterns from Security; CI Status, Acceptance Criteria, and Verification Commands from Correctness; Test Quality (combining Repetitive Test Detection, Test Documentation, and Eval Quality) and Test Change Classification from Style/Conventions
assertion_results.evidence: report.md summary table contains all required verdicts: Scope Containment (PASS), Diff Size (PASS), Commit Traceability (PASS) from Intent Alignment; Sensitive Patterns (PASS) from Security; CI Status (PASS), Acceptance Criteria (PASS), Verification Commands (N/A) from Correctness; Test Quality (PASS) with combined sub-verdicts ('All 4 test functions have documentation comments. No repetitive test functions detected... Eval Quality: N/A') and Test Change Classification (ADDITIVE) from Style/Conventions.

assertion_results.text: The report includes detailed findings with specific evidence for each domain — file-by-file scope comparison (Intent Alignment), line-level pattern scanning results (Security), per-criterion code-level verification (Correctness), and test quality assessment (Style/Conventions) — not just pass/fail verdicts in the summary table
assertion_results.evidence: The report provides detailed per-criterion code-level verification for Correctness (Acceptance Criteria Details table with 5 rows of reasoning, plus criterion-1.md through criterion-5.md with extensive code analysis). Test Quality assessment is present ('All 4 test functions have documentation comments. No repetitive test functions detected'). However, Intent Alignment lacks a file-by-file scope comparison — Scope Containment only has a one-line summary listing files without a structured expected-vs-actual comparison. Security lacks line-level pattern scanning results — Sensitive Patterns only states 'No secrets, credentials, API keys, or other sensitive patterns detected in added lines across all 3 files' without showing which patterns were scanned or any line-level detail. These are summary-level statements, not detailed findings with specific evidence.

assertion_results.text: Eval Quality is N/A because no eval result reviews exist in the PR — no reviews match the eval result detection criteria, so Eval Quality does not affect the Test Quality combination
assertion_results.evidence: report.md Test Quality row states 'Eval Quality: N/A.' Test Quality overall is PASS, confirming Eval Quality's N/A status did not negatively affect the combination.

assertion_results.text: No eval failure sub-tasks are created because Eval Quality is N/A — eval failure sub-tasks are only created when Eval Quality is WARN (at least one eval assertion failed)
assertion_results.evidence: Eval Quality is N/A (report.md Test Quality row: 'Eval Quality: N/A'). No sub-tasks are mentioned in any output file. Root-Cause Investigation row confirms 'No sub-tasks created; nothing to investigate'.

assertion_results.text: Review Feedback and Root-Cause Investigation verdicts are determined by the orchestrator independently from domain analysis — Review Feedback is N/A because no review comments exist, Root-Cause Investigation is N/A because no sub-tasks were created
assertion_results.evidence: report.md shows 'Review Feedback | N/A | No reviews or comments exist on the PR' and 'Root-Cause Investigation | N/A | No sub-tasks created; nothing to investigate'. These appear as independent top-level rows in the summary table, not grouped under any domain sub-agent (Intent Alignment, Security, Correctness, or Style/Conventions), consistent with orchestrator-level determination.
